summary: The file locus gains the `local` commitment class: layout-only, emit writes nothing there, and its members' rows never enter the lock — they derive at read time under the kind the lock declares

edit.description: The `Locus` union (75-78) spells three cases today — `at` / `embedded` / `nested-file` — and carries no commitment class. The `at` spelling gains the optional `local` commitment (the corpus's **file** locus is the SDK's `at`); its prose (63-69) and the `locus` field doc (147-148) state the class: per-machine and uncommitted, the kind declared and reviewed, the members' documents not.
The ts-rs seam row, regenerated from the Rust struct — do not hand-edit; `tests/seam_bindings_current.rs` holds it current.

`Governs` (30-38) carries root+glob only; the commitment class rides it or `CustomKind` (`governs` at 62, `with_locus` at 652, the row lift at 714-717). A local locus is **layout-only**: a local-locus kind whose content is not a declared layout is inadmissible — the corpus's own words, and the coupling that makes check-side-ness true by construction rather than a rule to enforce.

`KindFactRow` (2406) gains the commitment column. `emit` (840) / `emit_one` (1712) write nothing at a local member's path and record no provenance or emit-hash row for it — the lock captures the committed harness alone, so committed bytes stay layer-invariant by construction. The reap must not treat a local path as an ownerless projection (`emit_owned_targets`, 2304).

Check derives a local member's declaration rows at read time under the kind the committed lock declares, rather than reading them from the lock (the `admissibility` call sites at 811/826/903 are the seam). The engine-side `Locus` (`src/engine.rs`:133) is a different noun — a clause's binding grain, Document vs Embedded — and is not what this entry touches.

schemaDelta: KindFactRow gains an optional commitment column (`local`); a local-locus member contributes no provenance/emit-hash row to the lock.

tests.asserts: a local-locus member's document is read at check under the declared layout and gates; emit writes nothing at its path, records no row for it, and never reaps it; a local-locus kind that is not layout-content is inadmissible

acceptance: A local-locus kind is declarable, its members' documents govern at check and are invisible to emit and to the lock (representation.md, "locus"). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: First of 0032's five derivations; the class the other four rest on. Its `layout-only` fence is ratified text, but the dial — the first local-locus kind — is specified as TOML, which no layout can hold: see `(local-locus-toml-face)`. That fork rules the dial's face, not this class; if it widens `layout-only`, the fence re-cuts here. scoped at f1d97e4.

summary: A clause's compiled label becomes its address — deterministic, human-legible, written once at emit, printed by every finding and by `explain`; two rows under one label are a malformed lock

edit.description: `ClauseRow` (2548) carries kind/predicate/field/severity/guidance/cite and per-predicate argument columns — **no label column exists**. It gains one, written once at emit (840) as the row's identity. This is the entry's core: the label is not a spelling widened but a column added.

`Selector::rule` (348-353) is today's printed clause id and returns the bare predicate key (`max_len`) or `requirement.<key>` — not unique per row, since two clauses of one predicate over different fields of one kind collide. Widen it to the deterministic per-row address the lock writes; `finding` (548) already routes it onto every diagnostic. One identity per row: the label IS the rule id, never a second human-facing name beside it (0032's rejected alternative; `specs/process/engineering.md`, "One job, one home").
`Diagnostic::rule` (39-40, doc'd `skill.name-format` — stable, the diagnostic `code`) is the one home for the printed id; its doc states the label grammar.
The clause-row read-back (`clause_from_row`, 166-190) lifts the label off the row; the engine treats it as opaque and never resolves a collision.
`explain` (208) prints each clause's label — see the annoying finding, read its label, dial it.

Admissibility refuses two rows wearing one label — a malformed lock, rejected before it judges anything (`contract.md`, "well-formedness"). Sibling of the shipped `KIND_COLLISION_RULE` (1713) and `GOVERNS_COLLISION_RULE` (1787), which guard kind-name and governs-glob collisions; neither covers clause rows.
The ts-rs seam row, regenerated — do not hand-edit.

schemaDelta: ClauseRow gains a `label` column — the compiled address, written once at emit.

tests.asserts: every emitted clause row carries a deterministic, human-legible label; the same program emits the same labels; a lock carrying two rows under one label fails admissibility loud
a clause finding prints its label as the diagnostic code, and `explain` narrates the same label for the same clause

acceptance: Every clause declaration row has a documented, deterministic, human-legible address; every finding and `explain` print it; a colliding lock is refused (contract.md, "clause"; pipeline.md, "The lock"). `cargo test` green.
notes: 0032 assumed this shipped ('the lock already writes one per row and refuses collisions'); on disk it does not — the founding corpus claimed the label at e842a32 and no entry ever built it. So the work is the column plus the grammar plus the refusal, not a spelling change. Serialized behind the locus class: both edit `src/drift.rs` and `src/main.rs`. The dial cannot name a clause until this lands. scoped at f1d97e4.

summary: A policy layer is a lock the invocation joins: `check --layer <path>` joins named locks' rows over this corpus, kinds travel by name, and a joined lock failing admissibility fails the check

edit.description: The `Check` command (105-119: `root`, `harness`, `deny_advisories`, `reporter`) gains a repeatable `--layer <path>`. Each named lock's declaration rows join over the host corpus: kinds travel by name, the joined clauses range over the host's selections. Fail-closed costs nothing new — a joined lock that fails admissibility is already a malformed lock, refused loud through the existing `admissibility` path (811/826). 0030's uncommitted rules bind the join: hardening is unbounded in every mode, softening is visible-only and gate-inert.

`read_declarations` (3025) / `parse_declarations` (3048) read one lock today; the join reads the invocation's locks the same way — the lock stays the one interchange, and no second parser is minted beside this one.
Clause rows compose into the host's selections whatever lock carried them; a joined row is an ordinary row.

tests.asserts: `check --layer <lock>` joins the named lock's clauses over the host corpus and gates on them; a joined lock that fails admissibility fails the check loud; a joined clause that would soften the host's gate is inert in block mode while a hardening one binds

acceptance: An org authors an ordinary temper corpus, emits its lock, and distributes it; a consumer joins it at check and is gated by it, fail-closed (pipeline.md, "Layers"). `cargo test` green.
notes: The third of 0032's five. Org-side authoring stays TS and the lock stays the one interchange — no new format, no trust model, no payload-as-input (0032 rejects that by name: it breaks offline and the payload's internality). Serialized behind the label entry: both edit `src/main.rs`, `src/compose.rs`, `src/drift.rs`. scoped at f1d97e4.

summary: Ship the temper-owned `dial` kind: a local-locus document naming clauses by label and declaring the severity this machine reads them at — deletion unspellable, softening inert in block mode

edit.description: `all_kinds` (374-388) ships eleven claude-code kinds; the dial joins as temper's own — its schema IS 0030's envelope: an entry names a clause by its compiled label and declares a severity, and the schema cannot spell deletion, so a dialed clause still reports.

The provider face. The dial is **temper-owned, not a claude-code kind**, so it exports from the root, never the `claude-code` subpath (`specs/builtins.md`: the shipped claude-code kinds export from that subpath alone).
The root export carries the dial.
`Format` (507-522) spells `yaml-frontmatter` and `json-document` — no TOML face exists. What the dial's document face becomes is exactly the fork this entry waits on.
Check applies the dial's severities to the labelled clauses it names, and its softening is inert in block mode — so a block-mode pass on any machine implies the shared gate's pass.

schemaDelta: Pending the fork — the dial's document face is what `(local-locus-toml-face)` rules.

tests.asserts: a dial entry re-reads a clause at the declared severity and the clause still reports; a dial cannot spell deletion; dialed softening is inert in block mode while dialed hardening binds in every mode

acceptance: See the annoying finding, read its label, dial it — locally, uncommitted, visibly, and without ever softening the shared gate (contract.md, "clause"; pipeline.md, "Layers"). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Fork-gated, not merely serialized: `contract.md` calls the dial a local-locus **TOML** document while `representation.md` declares a local locus layout-only, and a layout is markdown's heading tree. Build must not pick between those sentences. Also serialized behind the join (shares `src/main.rs`) and needs the label to name a clause at all. scoped at f1d97e4.

summary: `check` announces every active local member, every dialed clause, and every joined lock — the gate says out loud which inputs judged this run

edit.description: The check run announces the three uncommitted-or-joined inputs by name: each active local member, each dialed clause (by its label), each joined lock. The announcement is unconditional — a run judged by inputs review never saw says so, whatever the verdict.

Every reporter renders one diagnostic source (`specs/distribution.md`): `session_start` (65), `github` (141), `sarif` (172) each carry the announcement in their own shape rather than one reporter growing a private line.

tests.asserts: a check run with a local member, a dialed clause, and a joined lock announces all three; a run with none announces nothing extra

acceptance: `check` announces every active local member, every dialed clause, and every joined lock (pipeline.md, "Layers"). `cargo test` green.
notes: Last of 0032's five — it announces what the other four introduce, so it is honest only once they exist. Serialized behind the dial (shares `src/main.rs`) and inherits its fork through the chain. scoped at f1d97e4.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. All cites re-verified unmoved at f1d97e4. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (65) sits under a doc (61-64) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (627) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (654), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 525 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 624 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 649-652 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth').

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1356, re-verified unmoved at f1d97e4) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1357-1361) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1359) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at f1d97e4 and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics; the window touches no import mechanics (`git log 9409a6c..HEAD -- src/ tests/` is empty). Every cite re-read on disk, all unmoved: the constant 5 at 65, the narration at 525/624, the BFS at 654, the boundary test at 1356. Stays parked: the constant may still be wrong. rescoped 4a33911; re-cited 53519b9; re-verified f1d97e4.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at f1d97e4, holding on every clause: `git tag -l` carries only the four era tags and no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, and release.yml:7-9 still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. The 9409a6c..HEAD window touched no release surface. scoped at b676656.
